title: React Hooks Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of useState, useEffect, and core rules for functional components in React.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primary purpose of the useState hook?
options: To define global variables.
To set or return a piece of local state in a functional component.
To manage component lifecycle methods.
To perform side effects like fetching data.

questions.question: Which hook should you use for side effects like data fetching or manual DOM updates?
options: useState()
useEffect()
useContext()
useReducer()

questions.question: What happens when you pass an empty dependency array ([]) to useEffect()?
options: It runs after every re-render.
It runs only once on mount.
It never runs.
It causes an error.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key rule of hooks in React?
options: Hooks can be called inside loops.
Hooks must be called at the top level of a React functional component.
Hooks are only for class components.
Hooks can only be used with single quotes.

questions.question: Does useState() always return an array with TWO items?
options: Yes, the current state value and the state-setter function.
No, it can return up to five items.
Yes, but only in ES6 or later.
No, it's an object in older browsers.
